contribution.title: Bekenstein entropy
contribution.description: Proposed that black holes carry entropy proportional to their horizon area: S = (k_B c^3 / 4 hbar G) A.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Defines the thermodynamic interpretation of black holes used in the Hawking-radiation pedagogy modules.

contribution.title: Bekenstein bound
contribution.description: Maximum amount of information that can be contained within a given region of space-time.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Speculative_extension territory. Useful for reasoning about holographic limits.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Thermodynamic gedankenexperiments; use the second law as a constraint.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What does the second law of thermodynamics say must happen here?
What information is hidden behind the horizon?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Conflating well-established formulas (horizon area entropy) with conjectural bounds.

reference.citation: Bekenstein, J. D. (1973). Black holes and entropy. Phys. Rev. D 7, 2333.

how_to_apply.cautions: Keep the analytic entropy formula separate from speculative quantum-gravity microstate counting.
